description: Questo sub-concetto descrive lo stato mentale, in particolare il grado di attenzione e concentrazione. La calma è spesso associata a una mente diffusa e rilassata, mentre l'allerta implica una concentrazione intensa.
positive: My mind drifts, like *nebulosa* dust, across the vastness of the afternoon sun.
Sentimenti sbiaditi, *pensées vagabondes*, a quiet acceptance of the gentle breeze.
Il pensiero si disperde, *un esprit libre*, come acqua che scorre tra le dita.
Una sensazione di calma, *laisser aller*, a feeling of lightness in my chest.
My awareness expands, *sans effort*, a gentle diffusion throughout my limbs.
Sento il mio sguardo *flotter*, a quiet observation without judgement, in the garden.
The thoughts fade, *wie eine Wolke*, a sense of spaciousness in my head.
Mi sento *desconectado*, a gentle release of mental tension, a quiet stillness.
Una percezione *diluita*, a sense of being present without grasping, just being.
My focus dissolves, *se disperse*, a peaceful surrender to the moment, simply existing.
Sento il pensiero *dériver*, a gentle flow of consciousness, without resistance, in the pool.
The mental chatter subsides, *tranquillitas animi*, a quiet openness to sensation, in the field.
Una sensazione di *fluir*, a gentle release of control, a sense of spaciousness within.
My attention wanders, *sans but*, a quiet observation of the passing clouds, in the sky.
Il pensiero si allenta, *wie ein Fluss*, a feeling of ease and acceptance, in the forest.
Sento il *desprendimiento*, a gentle letting go of mental effort, a sense of peace.
My awareness expands, *libre comme l'air*, a quiet observation of the world around me.
Una sensazione di *abertura*, a gentle receptivity to sensations, without judgment.
The mental grip loosens, *sin esfuerzo*, a feeling of lightness and spaciousness within.
Sento il *fluir* del pensiero, a gentle surrender to the present moment, without resistance.
negative: The focus tightens, *angulus in mente*, a sharp awareness of a single, tiny detail.
Sento la mente *concentrada*, a feeling of pressure behind my eyes, observing the bee.
My thoughts converge, *avec précision*, a feeling of intensity in my forehead, observing the ant.
Una sensazione di *tensione*, a feeling of being locked into a single point of awareness.
My awareness narrows, *stark und klar*, a feeling of being drawn inward, observing the stone.
Sento il pensiero *fissato*, a feeling of effort in my temples, observing the leaf.
The mental energy intensifies, *rigor*, a feeling of strain in my jaw, observing the flower.
Mi sento *obnubilato*, a feeling of being trapped within my own thoughts, observing the dew.
Una percezione *acuta*, a feeling of being pulled towards a single point, observing the spider.
My attention locks, *sin distracción*, a feeling of pressure in my skull, observing the insect.
Sento il pensiero *focalizzato*, a feeling of effort in my brow, observing the shell.
The mental field contracts, *in extremis*, a feeling of being driven by a singular purpose, observing the root.
Una sensazione di *rigidità*, a feeling of being unable to let go, observing the seed.
My awareness sharpens, *avec intensité*, a feeling of being trapped within a narrow scope, observing the moss.
Il pensiero si *stringe*, *wie ein Knoten*, a feeling of pressure in my head, observing the bark.
Sento il *focus*, a feeling of being unable to see beyond the immediate, observing the lichen.
My attention constricts, *sans relâche*, a feeling of being drawn inward, observing the fungus.
Una sensazione di *precisione*, a feeling of being unable to relax, observing the lichen.
The mental boundaries harden, *sin flexibilidad*, a feeling of being trapped by focus, observing the stone.
Sento il pensiero *dirigito*, a feeling of being unable to wander, observing the root.
